title: Apply bounded cache, Docker and trash retention
goal: Add guarded maintenance for user caches, Docker build cache, unused images, Grabowski releases, logs and trash with explicit exclusions for volumes, active builds and pinned recovery state.

approval.note: Source implementation is authorized. Applying cleanup to live caches or Docker requires exact-target dry-run evidence; Docker volumes remain outside generic authorization.

acceptance.assertion: Every maintenance class has a deterministic dry-run report before effects.
Docker volumes and running or referenced images are never included in generic cleanup.
Cache targets and age rules are policy-driven and idempotent.
Each applied cleanup records before and after sizes, exact targets and exclusions.

metadata.source: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1 plan from live 2026-07-14 storage audit
does_not_establish: permission to bypass leases or dirty-state guards
metadata.readiness_basis: STORAGE-LIFECYCLE-V1-T005 verified by merged Grabowski PR #222, current-runtime ancestry and successful live narrow-coordination evidence.
validation: 109 repository tests and syntax validation passed on the implementation.
GitHub validate, guard and smoke checks passed on PR #48.
Current clean Heim-PC main and origin/main both read back as the exact PR #48 merge commit.

verified_acceptance.dry-run: The current no-write run produced a deterministic hash-bound plan in 0.375 seconds and executed no apply.
verified_acceptance.docker-safety: Docker volumes are absent from the maintenance class contract; container-referenced and named images are exclusions, not generic cleanup candidates.
verified_acceptance.limits: Cache targets, age rules, pins and journal retention are policy-driven; repeated planning is read-only and current incomplete process observation fails closed.
verified_acceptance.receipts: Apply requires the exact plan SHA, explicit candidate set and APPLY:<plan_id> confirmation, then records per-candidate before/after evidence; no apply receipt was claimed because no cleanup was executed.

completion.outcome: Heim-PC maintenance now separates observation, hash-bound plan and explicit effect; Docker volumes stay outside scope and uncertain process observations block cleanup.
